name: ACMP
description: CALM architecture for the Authentication Credential Management Platform based on the current requirements baseline, FRS, and technical specification.

nodes.name: Authentication Credential Management Platform
nodes.description: Central internal platform for service and client registration, credential lifecycle management, HMAC issuance, authorization metadata, and audit governance.

nodes.name: Authorized Administrator
nodes.description: Authorized internal administrator who manages clients, credentials, scopes, revocation, rotation, and related platform actions.

nodes.name: Admin Web Portal
nodes.description: Internal AdminLTE-based web portal used to administer services, credentials, and platform configuration.

nodes.name: Credential Management API
nodes.description: Management API that supports client registration, credential issuance, rotation, revocation, package issuance, and audit-related operations.

nodes.name: Protected Recipient Service
nodes.description: Internal protected service or business API that validates inbound HMAC requests and enforces credential scope and permission checks.

nodes.name: Client Service
nodes.description: Internal client service that signs outbound HMAC requests when calling protected services.

nodes.name: MiniKMS
nodes.description: Internal cryptographic component used for HMAC secret generation, envelope encryption, decryption, and key versioning.

nodes.name: Credential Persistence Store
nodes.description: SQL Server or PostgreSQL data store holding service and client records, credential metadata, HMAC detail records, and audit data.

nodes.name: Encrypted Credential Package
nodes.description: Encrypted file artifact issued per KeyId for recipient-side HMAC validation in EncryptedFile mode.

nodes.name: Encrypted Client Credential Package
nodes.description: Encrypted file artifact issued per KeyId for client-side outbound HMAC signing.

relationships.description: Manages clients, credentials, scopes, and platform operations using
Reads and persists client, credential, and audit data in
Uses cryptographic secret generation and protection services from
Issues recipient-side encrypted credential packages to
Issues client-side encrypted credential packages to
Reads recipient-side encrypted credential material from
Includes HMAC-related supporting components and issued artifacts

flows.name: Register Client Service
flows.description: Authorized administrator creates a new internal client or service record through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: Administrator starts client or service registration from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the create client request to the credential management API.
Credential management API stores the new client or service record.

flows.name: Update Client Service
flows.description: Authorized administrator updates client or service metadata through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: Administrator opens the client or service update action in the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the metadata update request to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists the updated client or service metadata.

flows.name: Disable Client Service
flows.description: Authorized administrator disables a client or service through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: Administrator starts the disable action for the client or service from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the disable request to the credential management API.
Credential management API updates persisted client or service status to disabled.

flows.name: Issue HMAC Credential
flows.description: Authorized administrator issues a new HMAC credential using the portal, management API, MiniKMS, and persistence store.

transitions.description: Administrator starts HMAC credential issuance from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the HMAC credential issuance request to the credential management API.
Credential management API requests HMAC secret generation and protection from MiniKMS.
Credential management API persists encrypted secret material and credential metadata.

flows.name: Rotate HMAC Credential
flows.description: Authorized administrator rotates an HMAC credential using the portal, management API, MiniKMS, and persistence store.

transitions.description: Administrator starts HMAC credential rotation from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the credential rotation request to the credential management API.
Credential management API requests new secret generation and protection from MiniKMS.
Credential management API persists rotated credential state and related metadata.

flows.name: Revoke HMAC Credential
flows.description: Authorized administrator revokes an HMAC credential through the portal and management API.

transitions.description: Administrator starts credential revocation from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the credential revocation request to the credential management API.
Credential management API persists revoked credential state.

flows.name: Issue Encrypted Credential Package
flows.description: Authorized administrator uses the portal and management API to issue a recipient-side encrypted credential package.

transitions.description: Administrator starts encrypted credential package issuance from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the issuance request to the credential management API.
Credential management API issues the recipient-side encrypted credential package.

flows.name: Validate Inbound HMAC Request (KmsBacked)
flows.description: Protected recipient service validates an inbound HMAC request using cache-first runtime validation with persistence and MiniKMS on cache miss.

transitions.description: Client service sends an HMAC-signed request to the protected recipient service.
Protected recipient service resolves credential metadata when runtime cache is missing or stale.
Protected recipient service decrypts protected secret material using MiniKMS when required.

flows.name: Validate Inbound HMAC Request (EncryptedFile)
flows.description: Protected recipient service validates an inbound HMAC request using an encrypted credential package file.

transitions.description: Client service sends an HMAC-signed request to the protected recipient service.
Protected recipient service loads and validates encrypted credential package contents for request validation.

flows.name: Sign Outbound HMAC Request
flows.description: Client service signs an outbound request using the client package and then sends the signed request to the protected recipient service.

transitions.description: Client service loads protected signing material from the encrypted client credential package.
Client service sends the signed request to the protected recipient service.

flows.name: Reload Recipient Credential Package
flows.description: Protected recipient service reloads recipient-side package state after package replacement or refresh.

transitions.description: Protected recipient service reads refreshed encrypted credential package contents.

flows.name: Reload Client Credential Package
flows.description: Client service reloads client-side package state after package replacement or refresh.

transitions.description: Client service reads refreshed encrypted client credential package contents.

flows.name: List Credential Metadata
flows.description: Authorized user lists credential metadata without exposing plaintext secrets.

transitions.description: Authorized user opens credential metadata listing in the admin portal.
Admin portal requests credential metadata from the credential management API.
Credential management API reads credential metadata from the persistence store.

flows.name: Issue Encrypted Client Credential Package
flows.description: Authorized administrator uses the portal and management API to issue a client-side encrypted credential package.

transitions.description: Administrator starts encrypted client credential package issuance from the admin portal.
Admin portal submits the client package issuance request to the credential management API.
Credential management API issues the client-side encrypted credential package.
